homepage.heroSubline: Durable solutions for Colorado's harsh climate. Locally owned, built to last through freeze-thaw cycles and heavy snow.
aboutText: Horizon Concrete has been serving Denver since 2012, delivering durable concrete work that stands up to Colorado's extreme weather. We started with a single truck and a commitment to craftsmanship—now we're the go-to crew for foundations, driveways, and patios across the Front Range.

We don't cut corners. Every mix is engineered for Denver's rapid temperature swings, every pour timed to avoid spring hailstorms. Our team wears Carhartts, not suits—we're tradesmen who show up ready to work.
When we're not pouring concrete, you'll find us volunteering at City Park cleanups or sponsoring local Little League teams. This is our home too.

valueProps.title: Colorado-strong mixes
valueProps.description: Specially formulated concrete that resists cracking from freeze-thaw cycles.

valueProps.title: No surprise pricing
valueProps.description: Upfront quotes with itemized costs—we honor them even when material prices spike.

valueProps.title: Historic district experts
valueProps.description: Experience working with Denver's older homes and preservation requirements.

valueProps.title: 5-year warranty
valueProps.description: Backed by the longest guarantee in the metro area.

homepage.ctaSubline: Schedule your free consultation—we'll assess your site and recommend the right solution.

services.name: Concrete Foundations
services.shortDescription: Engineered bases that withstand Denver's shifting soils.
services.description:
A foundation is only as strong as the ground beneath it—and Denver's expansive clay soils demand special attention. We dig deeper footings for frost protection, use fiber-reinforced concrete to prevent hairline cracks, and install vapor barriers to combat basement moisture.

For historic homes in Capitol Hill or Park Hill, we match original foundation profiles while upgrading structural integrity. New infill constructions get moisture-monitored pours with expansion joints sized for Colorado's temperature swings. Every project includes post-pour curing blankets to prevent winter cracking.
process: Step 1: Soil testing and frost depth evaluation
Step 2: Custom formwork construction with rebar grid
Step 3: Controlled pour with vibration settling
Step 4: 7-day curing process with temperature monitoring
benefits: Prevents uneven settling in expansive soils

faq.question: How deep should footings be in Denver?
faq.answer: Minimum 36 inches to get below frost line, but we often go deeper in clay-heavy areas like Littleton. Soil tests determine final depth.

faq.question: Do you work with historic preservation boards?
faq.answer: Yes—we've completed approved foundations for 19th-century homes in Baker and Cheesman Park while maintaining original aesthetics.

services.name: Driveway Installation
services.shortDescription: Heavy-duty driveways built for plows and hail.
services.description:
Denver driveways face a triple threat: snowplow blades, hailstorms, and oil stains from mountain-bound SUVs. Our 6-inch-thick pours use 4,500 psi concrete with broom finishes that provide winter traction without shredding snowblower paddles.

In Highlands Ranch's planned communities, we match HOA requirements for decorative borders. For older Arvada ranches, we slope driveways properly to avoid ice dams. Every installation includes expansion joints at precise intervals to prevent unsightly cracks from temperature swings. Need snowmelt? We embed heating cables during pours for worry-free winters.
process: Step 1: Subgrade compaction with laser-level verification
Step 2: Gravel base layer with proper drainage slope
Step 3: Steel reinforcement grid installation
Step 4: Power-troweled finish for maximum density

faq.question: How soon can I park on a new driveway?
faq.answer: 7 days for passenger vehicles, 28 days for RVs. We'll provide a curing timeline specific to weather conditions.

faq.question: Do you repair existing driveways?
faq.answer: Yes—we assess whether partial replacement or full removal is needed, especially common with 1970s Lakewood homes where tree roots have caused damage.

services.name: Patio Pavers
services.shortDescription: Custom outdoor living spaces for Colorado lifestyles.
services.description:
From Wash Park bungalows to RiNo loft complexes, we build paver patios that handle Denver's extremes. Our base layers include 8 inches of compacted gravel with polymeric sand joints that resist weed growth and freeze damage.

We source locally from Rocky Mountain Stone to reduce transportation costs, offering tumbled pavers that mimic Red Rocks' hues for natural aesthetics. For Victorian homes, we replicate historic brick patterns. Modern designs get geometric layouts with integrated drainage channels to handle summer cloudbursts. All installations include a 2% slope away from structures to prevent foundation water damage.
process: Step 1: Excavation with proper slope calculations
Step 2: Geotextile fabric and gravel base installation
Step 3: Precision paver cutting and placement
Step 4: Joint sand activation and sealing
benefits: Permeable options meet Denver Water requirements
No hot-surface issues like concrete in summer

faq.question: Can pavers handle Denver's hail?
faq.answer: Yes—our tumbled concrete pavers have compressive strengths over 8,000 psi. We've installed them at Red Rocks-area homes with zero hail damage reports.

faq.question: How do you prevent frost heave?
faq.answer: Our deeper base layers include crushed granite that drains better than typical road base, critical for Littleton's clay-heavy soils.

services.name: Concrete Repair
services.shortDescription: Restoring damaged slabs without full replacement.
services.description:
Denver's freeze-thaw cycles wreck concrete—but total replacement isn't always necessary. Our structural epoxy injections fill cracks from the ground up, while polymer-modified overlights restore spalled surfaces on garage floors and sidewalks.

For historic districts like LoDo, we carefully remove and replace individual sections to maintain architectural integrity. Modern homes get color-matched repairs that blend seamlessly. We also stabilize sunken slabs with polyurethane foam jacketing, a minimally invasive solution perfect for Aurora's expansive soil neighborhoods. Every repair includes moisture testing to address underlying issues.
process: Step 1: Damage assessment with core sampling
Step 2: Preparation including rust removal from rebar
Step 3: Precision material application (epoxy, overlay, or foam)
Step 4: Texture matching and curing

faq.question: Can hairline cracks be repaired?
faq.answer: Yes—we inject low-viscosity epoxy that wicks into cracks as thin as 0.002 inches, common in older Arvada slab foundations.

faq.question: How long do overlays last?
faq.answer: Our polymer-modified systems typically last 10-15 years—we warranty them for 5 years against delamination or cracking.

services.name: Stamped Concrete
services.shortDescription: Decorative finishes that endure Colorado's elements.
services.description:
Want the look of flagstone without the maintenance? Our stamped concrete replicates natural materials while providing superior durability against Denver's hail and de-icing salts. Using release agents from Scofield, we create realistic textures ranging from ashlar slate to weathered wood.

For Highlands Ranch backyards, we integrate radiant heat cables before stamping. Wash Park historic homes get custom antique finishes that complement original architecture. All projects include penetrating sealers refreshed every 2-3 years to prevent UV damage and salt corrosion—critical near mountain-facing properties where winter chemicals are heavily used.
process: Step 1: Color hardener broadcast for consistent hue
Step 2: Precision stamping with alignment guides
Step 3: Joint cutting matching pattern lines
Step 4: Dual-layer sealing process
benefits: Custom colors match Denver Red rocks

faq.question: Does stamped concrete get slippery?
faq.answer: We add non-slip additives to all exterior applications—our textured finishes actually provide better traction than smooth pavers when wet.

faq.question: Can you stamp over existing concrete?
faq.answer: Sometimes—we'll core test your slab and may recommend a bond breaker layer first. Works best on pre-1990s concrete that hasn't been sealed with acrylics.

reviews.name: Mark T.
reviews.text: Horizon replaced our crumbling 1980s driveway in Lakewood with a heated concrete slab. Their crew worked through a spring snowstorm to finish before my daughter's wedding. Two winters later—not a single crack despite my plow guy's heavy blade.

reviews.name: Lisa R.
reviews.text: The patio crew matched our RiNo loft's industrial aesthetic perfectly with large-format stamped concrete that looks like weathered steel. Even after last July's hail storm (you know the one), there wasn't a single chip. These guys understand Denver weather.

reviews.name: James K.
reviews.text: As a structural engineer, I inspected Horizon's foundation work on my own 1920s Park Hill home. Their rebar spacing and vapor barrier installation were textbook perfect. Most impressive was how they compacted the clay soil—no settlement cracks after 18 months.

reviews.name: Sarah L.
reviews.text: Our Wash Park bungalow needed a patio that wouldn't flood during summer storms. Horizon graded the pavers to drain toward the alley and used permeable joints. Now we're the only house on the block without standing water after heavy rains.

reviews.name: Derek M.
reviews.text: Thought we'd need to replace our entire Aurora garage floor after salt damage. Horizon's overlay system made it look new for a fraction of the cost. They even matched the original broom finish so perfectly my HOA didn't notice the repair.

reviews.name: Maria G.
reviews.text: Horizon navigated Highlands Ranch HOA requirements flawlessly for our circular driveway. Their crew repaired sprinkler lines they uncovered at no extra charge. The control joints are cut exactly where predicted—no random cracks like our neighbors have.

service-areas.title: Denver Concrete Service Areas | Horizon CO
service-areas.description: Local concrete contractors serving Denver, Aurora, Lakewood, Arvada, Littleton, Highlands Ranch. Residential and commercial work.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Concrete proudly serves Aurora, Colorado, where many homes and businesses were built during the 1970s-80s suburban expansion. Our expertise in durable concrete solutions is perfect for Aurora's aging infrastructure, particularly for driveways, foundations, and patios that need reinforcement against heavy snow loads. We also specialize in modern concrete finishes that complement the mid-century aesthetic prevalent in many Aurora neighborhoods. With cold winters and frequent snowfall, our concrete work is designed to resist freeze-thaw cycles, ensuring longevity and structural integrity.
serviceAreas.localContext: Aurora features 1970s-80s suburban developments with a mix of mid-century architectural styles.

serviceAreas.content: Lakewood, Colorado, is home to many 1960s-70s split-level homes, which often require concrete repairs and upgrades. Horizon Concrete specializes in foundation stabilization, stamped concrete patios, and durable walkways that can withstand Lakewood's cold winters and heavy snowfall. Our team understands the unique challenges of preserving and enhancing older homes while incorporating modern concrete solutions. Whether it's a cracked driveway or a new decorative concrete feature, we provide reliable service tailored to Lakewood's residential needs.
serviceAreas.localContext: Lakewood is known for its 1960s-70s split-level homes and suburban charm.

serviceAreas.content: Arvada's 1960s-70s ranch-style homes often feature original concrete work that may need repair or replacement. Horizon Concrete offers expert services for foundations, driveways, and outdoor living spaces, ensuring they endure Colorado's harsh winters. Our concrete solutions are designed to blend seamlessly with Arvada's architectural character while providing modern durability. From decorative stamped concrete to essential structural repairs, we help homeowners maintain and enhance their properties with high-quality craftsmanship.
serviceAreas.localContext: Arvada is characterized by its 1960s-70s ranch homes and historic neighborhoods.
